zh.lifeSummary: 當Ida de Hastings於大約1212年出生在英國瓦維其可爾費隆格利時，她的父親William de Hastings48歲，母親Margaret le Bigod31歲。她於大約1230年與Stephen de Segrave在英國諾福爾可阿希爾結婚。她去世於1288年3月2日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年77歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦克里斯蒂 邱奇 格也福利亞斯 紐蓋特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Ida de Hastings于大约1212年出生在英国瓦維其可爾費隆格利时，她的父亲William de Hastings48岁，母亲Margaret le Bigod31岁。她于大约1230年与Stephen de Segrave在英国諾福爾可阿希爾结婚。她去世于1288年3月2日在英国米德萊可倫敦享年77岁，埋葬在英国英格兰倫敦克里斯蒂 邱奇 格也福利亞斯 紐蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Hastings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from Hastings (Sussex), near which the English army was defeated by the Normans in 1066. The placename derives from an Old English personal name Hǣsta + the Old English groupname suffix -ingas. In the latter part of the 12th century, at the time of William the Lion, the surname was taken to Scotland, where it assimilated instances of the native Scottish surname Harestane.
English: variant of Hasting with Middle English genitival -s.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hOistín ‘descendant of Oistín’, the Gaelic form of the personal name Augustine (see Austin ).

variants.name: Hasting
meanings: English: from the Norman personal name Hastang, Hastenc, Hasten (Old Norse Hásteinn ‘high’ + ‘stone’). See also Hastings .

variants.name: Heston
meanings: English: habitational name from Heston (Middlesex) or Histon (Cambridgeshire). The Middlesex placename derives from Old English hǣs ‘brushwood’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, while the Cambridgeshire placename may derive from Old English hyse ‘shoot, tendril’, also ‘son, youth’, + tūn.

nameMeanings.name: Ida
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Norman name, of Germanic origin, derived from īd ‘work’. This died out during the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, influenced by its use in Tennyson's The Princess ( 1847 ) for the central character, who devotes herself to the cause of women's rights and women's education in a thoroughly Victorian way. The name is also associated with Mount Ida in Crete, which was connected in classical times with the worship of Zeus, king of the gods, who was supposed to have been brought up in a cave on the mountainside. In the 1930s it became famous as the name of the film star Ida Lupino ( 1914–1995 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Ida de Hastings was born about 1212, in Fillongley, Warwickshire, England, her father, William de Hastings, was 48 and her mother, Margaret le Bigod, was 31. She married Stephen de Segrave about 1230, in Ashill, Norfolk, England. She died on 2 March 1288, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 77, and was buried in Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, London, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Stephen de Segrave environ 1230, à Ashill, Norfolk, Angleterre. Elle est décédée le 2 mars 1288, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans, et a été enterrée à Christ Church Greyfriars Newgate, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.Grey Friars, London, Middlesex, England: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크리스트 c후르치 그리프리아르스 뉴가트
ko.lifeSummary: Ida de Hastings(이)가 대략 1212년 영국 워윅실르 피론글리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Hastings(은)는 48세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret le Bigod(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1230년 영국 노르폴크 애시에서 Stephen de Segrave(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1288년 3월 2일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 77세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 크리스트 c후르치 그리프리아르스 뉴가트에 묻혔다.

ru.Grey Friars, London, Middlesex, England: краист чурч грифрярс нюгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Ida de Hastings родилась около 1212 г. в местности филлонгли, варвиккшир, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Hastings было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret le Bigod, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen de Segrave около 1230 г. в местности ашилл, норфолк, Англия. Она умерла 2 марта 1288 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 77 лет и была похоронена в местности краист чурч грифрярс нюгэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.
